factors.summary: Pricing power is present, with higher margins from larger projects and emergency restoration services.

evidence.text: Gross profit as a percentage of revenues increased to 12.2% for the three months ended March 31, 2017 from 11.9% for the three months ended March 31, 2016. The increases in gross profit and gross profit as a percentage of revenues were primarily due to the increase in revenues described above, as well as greater contributions from larger pipeline project margins and the incremental emergency restoration services revenues mentioned above, which both typically yield higher margins.
evidence.subsection: Three months ended March 31, 2017 compared to the three months ended March 31, 2016

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Higher margins from larger projects and emergency services indicate strong pricing power.

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting toward higher-margin segments, particularly in oil and gas infrastructure services.

evidence.text: Revenues for this segment increased $431.9 million, or 82.0%, to $958.7 million for the three months ended March 31, 2017. This increase was primarily the result of increased capital spending by our customers on larger pipeline transmission projects, as certain of these projects, after experiencing regulatory and permitting delays, moved into full construction during the second half of 2016 and remained in full construction during the first quarter of 2017.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Shifting towards higher-margin segments like oil and gas infrastructure services.

factors.summary: Unit volumes are growing, particularly in oil and gas infrastructure services, driven by increased project activity.

evidence.text: Revenues for this segment increased $431.9 million, or 82.0%, to $958.7 million for the three months ended March 31, 2017. This increase was primarily the result of increased capital spending by our customers on larger pipeline transmission projects, as certain of these projects, after experiencing regulatory and permitting delays, moved into full construction during the second half of 2016 and remained in full construction during the first quarter of 2017.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Growing unit volumes driven by increased project activity.

factors.summary: Gross and operating margins are expanding, driven by higher-margin projects and revenue growth.

evidence.text: Gross profit as a percentage of revenues increased to 12.2% for the three months ended March 31, 2017 from 11.9% for the three months ended March 31, 2016. The increases in gross profit and gross profit as a percentage of revenues were primarily due to the increase in revenues described above, as well as greater contributions from larger pipeline project margins and the incremental emergency restoration services revenues mentioned above, which both typically yield higher margins.
evidence.subsection: Three months ended March 31, 2017 compared to the three months ended March 31, 2016

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Expanding margins due to higher-margin projects and revenue growth.

factors.summary: The company is in growth mode, with increased hiring to support business expansion.

evidence.text: This increase was primarily attributable to $17.2 million in higher compensation costs, largely associated with higher incentive compensation based on current levels of profitability, as well as annual compensation increases and increased personnel to support business growth;
evidence.subsection: Three months ended March 31, 2017 compared to the three months ended March 31, 2016

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Increased hiring to support business expansion indicates growth.

factors.summary: Foreign currency exchange rates and weather conditions are key sensitivities impacting near-term results.

evidence.text: Foreign currency exchange rates also favorably impacted revenues by approximately $20 million during the three months ended March 31, 2017, primarily attributable to the strengthening of the Canadian dollar against the U.S. dollar. Adverse or favorable weather conditions can impact gross margins in a given period.
evidence.subsection: Three months ended March 31, 2017 compared to the three months ended March 31, 2016

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Mixed impact from favorable FX and weather risks.

factors.summary: Acquisitions are contributing to revenue growth, with approximately $10 million in revenues from acquired companies.

evidence.text: The increase in revenues from electric power infrastructure services resulted primarily from increased customer spending associated with larger transmission services, $18.7 million in additional emergency restoration services revenues and approximately $10 million in revenues from acquired companies.
evidence.subsection: Three months ended March 31, 2017 compared to the three months ended March 31, 2016

factors.summary: Foreign exchange movements are favorably impacting results, particularly from the strengthening of the Canadian dollar.

evidence.text: Foreign currency exchange rates also favorably impacted revenues by approximately $20 million during the three months ended March 31, 2017, primarily attributable to the strengthening of the Canadian dollar against the U.S. dollar. Similarly, the gain in the three months ended March 31, 2016 was due to a strengthening of foreign currencies associated with our international operations, primarily the Canadian and Australian dollars, against the U.S. dollar as of March 31, 2016 when compared to exchange rates for those same currencies as of December 31, 2015.
evidence.subsection: Three months ended March 31, 2017 compared to the three months ended March 31, 2016

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Favorable foreign exchange impacts results positively.

factors.summary: The company anticipates a strong backlog and active spending over the next few years.

evidence.text: The timing and scope of projects can also be affected by other factors such as siting, right-of-way and unfavorable economic and market conditions. We anticipate many of these issues to be overcome and spending on transmission projects to be active over the next few years, and we currently have a number of these projects underway and expect this segment's backlog to remain strong throughout 2017.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company anticipates a strong backlog and active spending, indicating positive future demand.

factors.summary: The company is taking steps to maintain pricing discipline and manage competitive pressures.

evidence.text: We will remain focused on maintaining our pricing discipline and believe competitive pressures could recede further as large, high-voltage transmission projects move forward and the small and medium size transmission market continues to grow and/or contractors adjust further to the current environment.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company is taking steps to manage competitive pressures, which could improve margins.

factors.summary: The company's long-term strategic direction is clear, focusing on renewable energy, transmission infrastructure, and international growth opportunities.

evidence.text: We consider renewable energy, including solar and wind generation facilities, to be an ongoing opportunity for our engineering, project management and installation services. Concerns about greenhouse gas emissions, as well as the goal of reducing reliance on power generation from fossil fuels, have created demand for more renewable energy sources.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company's focus on renewable energy and international growth shows a strong strategic direction.

factors.summary: Demand is accelerating, particularly in oil and gas infrastructure services and electric power infrastructure services. Additionally, long-term growth in electricity demand is expected, driven by aging infrastructure and renewable energy developments.

evidence.text: Revenues increased $464.4 million, or 27.1%, to $2.18 billion for the three months ended March 31, 2017. Contributing to the increase was a $431.9 million increase in revenues from oil and gas infrastructure services and a $32.5 million increase in revenues from electric power infrastructure services.
evidence.subsection: Three months ended March 31, 2017 compared to the three months ended March 31, 2016

evidence.text: Demand for electricity in North America is expected to grow over the long term. Certain segments of the North American electric power grid are not adequate to efficiently serve the power needs of the future.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Demand is accelerating in key segments, driven by infrastructure needs and renewable energy growth.

factors.summary: The company is experiencing accelerating organic revenue growth, driven by increased customer spending on larger projects, particularly in oil and gas infrastructure services and electric power infrastructure services. Additionally, the company anticipates continued organic revenue growth due to long-term demand for transmission and renewable energy infrastructure as power generation needs expand.

evidence.text: Revenues increased $464.4 million, or 27.1%, to $2.18 billion for the three months ended March 31, 2017. Contributing to the increase was a $431.9 million increase in revenues from oil and gas infrastructure services and a $32.5 million increase in revenues from electric power infrastructure services.
evidence.subsection: Three months ended March 31, 2017 compared to the three months ended March 31, 2016

evidence.text: We expect these system upgrades could result in increased spending and increased demand for our services over the long term. As demand for power grows, the need for new power generation facilities is expected to grow.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: The company is experiencing significant organic revenue growth across multiple segments.

factors.summary: The end market momentum is positive, with growth driven by increased capital spending on larger pipeline projects in the oil and gas infrastructure services segment, as well as growth in renewable energy and natural gas distribution. Strategic relationships and system upgrades in response to severe weather events further contribute to this momentum.

evidence.text: Revenues for this segment increased $431.9 million, or 82.0%, to $958.7 million for the three months ended March 31, 2017. This increase was primarily the result of increased capital spending by our customers on larger pipeline transmission projects, as certain of these projects, after experiencing regulatory and permitting delays, moved into full construction during the second half of 2016 and remained in full construction during the first quarter of 2017.

The need to ensure available specialized labor resources for projects also drives strategic relationships with customers. In addition, a number of utilities are implementing system upgrades or hardening programs in response to severe weather events that have occurred over the past few years, which is also increasing distribution investment in some regions of the United States.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Positive momentum driven by increased spending on larger projects and renewable energy growth.

factors.summary: Labor costs are increasing, primarily due to higher compensation costs and incentive compensation, which is creating margin pressure, particularly in the Canadian operations.

evidence.text: Selling, general and administrative expenses increased $26.0 million, or 16.4%, to $184.6 million for the three months ended March 31, 2017. This increase was primarily attributable to $17.2 million in higher compensation costs, largely associated with higher incentive compensation based on current levels of profitability, as well as annual compensation increases and increased personnel to support business growth;
evidence.subsection: Three months ended March 31, 2017 compared to the three months ended March 31, 2016

evidence.text: In the near-term, margins in our Electric Power Infrastructure Services segment have experienced pressure as construction of several large, high-voltage transmission projects has been delayed due to the challenging regulatory approval and permitting environment.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Increasing labor costs are putting pressure on margins, particularly in Canadian operations.

factors.summary: The company faces mixed near-term risks, including operational challenges such as weather delays and project completion uncertainties, as well as external factors like regulatory delays and economic conditions. These risks have already impacted project timelines and financial performance, particularly in the electric power infrastructure services segment.

evidence.text: During the three months ended March 31, 2017, we experienced weather delays on a natural gas transmission project that resulted in an increase of the estimated total costs necessary to complete the project. During the three months ended March 31, 2017, we recognized losses on the project of $16.5 million. We are in the process of developing potential claims; however, no revenues or cost recovery has been reflected in our estimate of total project losses at March 31, 2017.

The regulatory and environmental permitting processes remain a hurdle for some proposed transmission and renewable energy projects, and these factors continue to create uncertainty as to timing of this spending. In the near-term, our electric power infrastructure services operations have been impacted by regulatory delays, particularly for large transmission projects.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Near-term operational and regulatory challenges are impacting project timelines and performance.

factors.summary: Management exhibits a mixed sentiment, with confidence in increased investment in technology and business development initiatives, yet also shows cautious optimism regarding strategic opportunities in specific segments, acknowledging cyclicality and execution risks.

evidence.text: Management appears confident, with increased investment in technology and business development initiatives.
evidence.subsection: Three months ended March 31, 2017 compared to the three months ended March 31, 2016

evidence.text: Other increases primarily include additional costs related to the support of increased revenues and costs associated with ongoing technology and business development initiatives.
evidence.subsection: Oil and Gas Infrastructure Services Segment

evidence.text: Overall, we remain optimistic about this segment’s operations going forward.
evidence.subsection: Oil and Gas Infrastructure Services Segment

evidence.text: From a near- and medium-term perspective, we continue to believe that larger pipeline opportunities can provide significant profitability, although these projects and the profits they generate are often subject to more cyclicality and execution risk than our other service offerings.
evidence.subsection: Oil and Gas Infrastructure Services Segment

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Management shows confidence in investments and optimism about growth, despite acknowledging risks.

factors.summary: Construction activity and infrastructure spending are growing, particularly in oil and gas infrastructure services and electric power infrastructure. The growth is driven by increased capital spending on larger pipeline transmission projects and renewable energy and transmission upgrades.

evidence.text: Revenues for this segment increased $431.9 million, or 82.0%, to $958.7 million for the three months ended March 31, 2017. This increase was primarily the result of increased capital spending by our customers on larger pipeline transmission projects, as certain of these projects, after experiencing regulatory and permitting delays, moved into full construction during the second half of 2016 and remained in full construction during the first quarter of 2017.

Demand for electricity in North America is expected to grow over the long term. Certain segments of the North American electric power grid are not adequate to efficiently serve the power needs of the future.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Construction and infrastructure spending are growing significantly, driven by capital investments in key projects.

factors.summary: Larger pipeline projects, emergency restoration services, the development of renewable energy projects, and the implementation of FERC Order No. 1000 are acting as catalysts for growth. These factors contribute to higher margins and more efficient development of electric transmission facilities.

evidence.text: The increases in gross profit and gross profit as a percentage of revenues were primarily due to the increase in revenues described above, as well as greater contributions from larger pipeline project margins and the incremental emergency restoration services revenues mentioned above, which both typically yield higher margins.
evidence.subsection: Three months ended March 31, 2017 compared to the three months ended March 31, 2016

evidence.text: The Federal Energy Regulatory Commission (FERC) issued FERC Order No. 1000 to promote more efficient and cost-effective development of new transmission facilities. The order establishes transmission planning and cost allocation requirements intended to facilitate multi-state electric transmission lines and to encourage competition by removing, under certain conditions, federal rights of first refusal from FERC-approved tariffs and agreements.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Multiple catalysts contribute to growth, including renewable energy projects and regulatory support.
